# fbsviewer-lib

> Interactive FlatBuffers and Protocol Buffers binary encoding visualizer. See
> what every byte means -- vtables, scalars, strings, vectors, structs, padding,
> tags, varints -- color-coded and cross-linked between hex view and structure tree.

Live demo: https://fbsviewer.shuozeli.com/
Source: https://github.com/Shuozeli/fbsviewer-lib
Deploy repo (pre-built WASM): https://github.com/Shuozeli/fbsviewer

## What This Tool Does

FlatBuffers binary encoding is opaque. Unlike Protocol Buffers (which are
length-prefixed and straightforward to decode), FlatBuffers uses vtables and
signed/unsigned offsets that make the byte layout non-obvious. This tool takes a
`.fbs` schema and a FlatBuffer (JSON or raw hex), compiles the schema, encodes
the data, and shows you the role of every byte.

Use cases:
- Debugging FlatBuffers encoding issues (fields reading as zero, garbled strings)
- Learning how FlatBuffers binary format works (vtables, offsets, alignment)
- Verifying binary compatibility between implementations
- Inspecting binaries produced by any FlatBuffers implementation (C++ flatc, etc.)

## Architecture

Three Rust crates in a Cargo workspace:

### visualizer-core (portable library, no GUI)

JSON encoder/decoder, hex parser, schema loader, and protobuf bridge. Re-exports
binary walker and region types from `flatc-rs-annotator`.

- `lib.rs` -- Re-exports walk_binary, AnnotatedRegion, RegionType from flatc-rs-annotator; re-exports ProtoSchema from protoc-rs-schema
- `json_encoder.rs` -- JSON -> FlatBuffers binary encoding (pure Rust)
- `json_decoder.rs` -- Annotated regions -> JSON decoding
- `proto_walker.rs` -- Bridge: protobuf-rs annotator -> shared AnnotatedRegion type for protobuf binary visualization
- `hex_parse.rs` -- Hex string -> bytes parser
- `schema_loader.rs` -- JSON string -> Schema loader

### visualizer (egui GUI app, native + WASM)

Interactive GUI built with egui. Compiles to native desktop (eframe) and
WebAssembly (trunk + wasm-bindgen). Uses MVU (Model-View-Update) architecture.

- `state.rs` -- Pure AppState, Command enum, Effect enum, dispatch() function; auto-detects FlatBuffers vs Protobuf schemas
- `view.rs` -- render_view() extracts egui rendering, emits Commands; responsive layout (narrow/wide)
- `app.rs` -- Thin egui shell: dispatch loop, effect execution; CJK font loading
- `hex_view.rs` -- Color-coded hex byte display with hover/click highlighting
- `structure_view.rs` -- Collapsible tree view of binary structure
- `templates.rs` -- 12 built-in example schemas (Monster, unions, vectors, etc.)
- `syntax.rs` -- FBS and JSON syntax highlighting for editor widgets
- `permalink.rs` -- Encode/decode state as URL-safe permalink (deflate + base64url)

### visualizer-cli (CLI binary inspection tool)

Command-line tool for inspecting FlatBuffers and Protobuf binaries without a GUI.

- Binary: `flatbuf-viz`
- Input: `.fbs`, `.json`, or `.proto` schema file + binary/hex data file
- Output: structured text showing byte regions, field values, and offsets
- Filters: `--byte-range`, `--field`, `--region-type`
- Output formats: `--format table` (default), `json`, `compact`

## Dependencies

Depends on [flatbuffers-rs](https://github.com/Shuozeli/flatbuffers-rs) for:
- `flatc-rs-annotator` -- Binary walker and region type definitions
- `flatc-rs-compiler` -- Schema compilation (.fbs -> parsed Schema)
- `flatc-rs-schema` -- Schema type definitions
- `flatc-rs-fbs-gen` -- Random schema generation (for template examples)
- `flatc-rs-data-gen` -- Random data generation (for template examples)

Depends on [protobuf-rs](https://github.com/Shuozeli/protobuf-rs) for:
- `protoc-rs-annotator` -- Protobuf binary annotation
- `protoc-rs-schema` -- Protobuf schema types (FileDescriptorSet)
- `protoc-rs-analyzer` -- Proto source compilation (.proto -> FileDescriptorSet)

## Build

```bash
# Native desktop app
cargo run -p flatbuf-visualizer

# Run tests (148 tests)
cargo test --workspace

# WASM web app
cd visualizer && trunk build --release --public-url ./
```

## Key Features

- Paste `.fbs` schema + JSON or hex data (FlatBuffers), or `.proto` schema + hex binary (Protobuf)
- Auto-detection of schema format (FlatBuffers vs Protobuf)
- Pure Rust JSON-to-FlatBuffers encoder (works on native and WASM)
- Hex view with per-byte coloring by region type
- Structure tree with collapsible hierarchy
- Bidirectional hover highlighting with click-to-lock
- Data format dropdown (JSON / Hex) with auto-conversion
- 12 built-in template examples
- Random schema and data generation
- Shareable permalinks (deflate + base64url encoded in URL)
- FBS and JSON syntax highlighting in editors
- Decoded JSON view
- File upload support (native: file dialogs, WASM: browser upload)
- Responsive layout (narrow: stacked, wide: side-by-side)
